Configuration Reference

Pagetomd reads configuration from three sources, applied in order: built-in defaults, environment variables prefixed with PAGETOMD_, and command-line flags. Later sources override earlier ones. This page documents every supported field, its type, its default value, and how to set it from each source.

Network configuration

Network options control how pagetomd talks to the origin server. They apply to the default httpx fetcher and, where meaningful, to the optional Playwright backend.

timeout

Maximum number of seconds to wait for any single HTTP request before giving up. Must be strictly positive. Applies to both the initial GET and to robots.txt lookups, although the latter is additionally capped at five seconds regardless of this value.

Setting from the CLI

Use the --timeout flag, e.g. pagetomd --timeout 5 https://example.com.

Setting from the environment

Export PAGETOMD_TIMEOUT in your shell:

export PAGETOMD_TIMEOUT=5
pagetomd https://example.com

retries

Number of additional attempts made after the initial request fails with a retryable error. Must be non-negative. Retries are only attempted for transport errors and for the HTTP status codes 408, 425, 429, 500, 502, 503, and 504. Any other failure is terminal.

from pagetomd.config import Config

cfg = Config.from_overrides({
    "url": "https://example.com",
    "retries": 5,
    "timeout": 10.0,
})
print(cfg.retries)  # 5

Conversion options

Conversion options affect how the extracted HTML is turned into Markdown. They have no effect on the network layer.

wide_tables

Strategy for rendering tables wider than the GFM threshold. Accepts one of three string values: kv, html, or drop. The default is kv.

ValueBehaviourWhen to use
kvRender each row as a key-value list.LLM consumption.
htmlPass the original <table> through unchanged.Human review of layout-sensitive data.
dropReplace the table with a one-line placeholder.You don't care about wide tables at all.

code_fences

When true (the default), code blocks render as fenced blocks with language hints inferred from the source HTML. When false, code blocks render as indented blocks. We strongly recommend leaving this enabled — fenced blocks are friendlier to syntax highlighters.

def example() -> None:
    """Demonstrate a fenced code block with a language hint."""
    print("hello world")

See also

For the higher-level pipeline overview, see the pipeline section below. For environment variable details, see environment variables.

Pipeline

The pipeline orchestrates fetch, extract, convert, and write in that order. Each stage is independently configurable; see the module documentation for details.

Environment variables

Every Config field can be set from an environment variable prefixed with PAGETOMD_. Boolean fields accept true, false, 1, 0, yes, no (case-insensitive). Numeric fields are parsed as floats or integers as appropriate.
